reasoning: The current 90-day AA Financial CP rate has fluctuated recently around 4.26-4.39, closing at 4.39 on Feb 28. This local high establishes a tough baseline for an increase in the short term. The short-term statistical trend provided by FRED estimates P(> 4.39) at 2025-03-09 to be 0.26, heavily driven by strong mean reversion factors and recent mild downward drift. Looking further out, markets currently assign a high probability (>75%) of at least two 0.25-point Fed rate cuts by the end of 2025 (likely commencing around June). Because CP rates price in anticipated borrowing costs over a 90-day window, these rates will drift significantly lower leading into anticipated rate cuts. Thus, the expected mean rate is projected to be much lower at the end of the year and into early 2026. However, as the forecast horizon extends, the macro uncertainty significantly expands (inflation could rebound, forcing the Fed to surprisingly hold or hike rates). This rising variance naturally increases the probability mass residing above 4.39 despite a lower expected mean. I follow the instruction to have uncertainty increase across the horizons, smoothly transitioning the probabilities closer to 0.5.
